prompt: In the image, a billiards table is shown with its pockets numbered 1 – 6 in red. If the blue ball rolls in the direction indicated by the green arrow—without spin and with unlimited momentum—and is guaranteed to drop into a pocket, which pocket will it enter? Please answer with the single digit 1 – 6.
dense_caption: A billiards table is shown with six red-numbered pockets. The blue ball has dominant body color RGB(100, 175, 220), hex #64AFDC. The direction arrow is a muted green, not a bright neon green, with dominant body color RGB(158, 215, 126), hex #9ED77E. Its corresponding HSV is approximately H=98.4°, S=41.4%, V=84.3%, or about OpenCV HSV (49, 106, 215). This green color value is intended to be accurate for this task family, appears only on the direction arrow, and can be used to filter or localize the arrow position. If arrow filtering or localization is needed, it must start from this provided color value rather than from a generic green heuristic. The green direction marker consists of a straight line segment plus an arrowhead; the straight line indicates the initial path direction starting from the ball-side origin point. A practical heuristic is to connect the centroid of the blue ball region and the centroid of the green arrow region, then extend that line until it reaches a rail to estimate the initial travel direction. The table boundary has multiple visible layers near the pockets and outer frame, including lighter gray regions and curved decorative shapes, but the effective reflection boundary is only the first darker straight rail edge that directly borders the white playing surface. Ignore the outer lighter layers and rounded pocket decorations when deciding reflections. When the trajectory hits a horizontal rail, it reflects vertically; when it hits a vertical rail, it reflects horizontally. The ball is near the right side of the table, and the arrow starts near the ball and points down-left toward the lower rail.
